# AI Coding Assistants Talk Track

Use this note as speaker guardrails. The talk should say the labour is shifting towards steering, review, proof, and governance, while avoiding claims that assistants make all developers faster or replace accountable review.

## Source Slice

- Source path: `research/hmrc-beyond-hype/import/AI coding assistants on 9 May 2026 for the HMRC Data Science Academy talk.md`.
- Source section: [What to say in the HMRC talk](../../import/AI%20coding%20assistants%20on%209%20May%202026%20for%20the%20HMRC%20Data%20Science%20Academy%20talk.md#what-to-say-in-the-hmrc-talk).
- Source line range in the current import file: `326-346`.
- Source status: point-in-time public-source snapshot dated 9 May 2026; recheck fast-moving product claims before live use.

## Talk Use

- Use the positive line: the repo shows human specification, constraints, docs, tests, evidence export, and security review working together.
- Use the boundary line: do not equate benchmark gains, enterprise licences, or PR-review bots with safe departmental deployment.
- Connect every claim back to evidence and provenance.
